factions.description_en: The first hidden village founded by Hashirama Senju and Madara Uchiha, Konoha is the central political and military power of the Land of Fire. Home to numerous illustrious clans, it has trained many of the most influential shinobi in history.

Hidden village of the Land of Wind, governed by the Kazekage, Sunagakure is a strategic partner of Konoha despite a historical rivalry. Gaara, a former jinchuuriki, exercises a decisive pacifying influence there.

Hidden village of the Land of Lightning, renowned for its military power and lightning techniques. Kumogakure maintained tensions with Konoha, notably during the attempted kidnapping of Hinata Hyuga, before becoming an ally of the great alliance.
Hidden village of the Land of Earth, led by the Tsuchikage, Iwagakure is marked by long-standing animosity toward Konoha. Under Onoki, it joined the Great Shinobi Alliance to face Madara and Obito.

Hidden village of the Land of Water, formerly governed by a Mizukage under Obito Uchiha's influence, known as the Bloody Village due to its brutal practices. Under Mei Terumi, Kirigakure undergoes profound reformation.

A small village perpetually at war, Amegakure was transformed into a theocratic state by Nagato under the identity of Pain. Yahiko founded the original Akatsuki there before being killed by Hanzo, which drove Nagato toward nihilism.

Village founded by Orochimaru as a cover for his forbidden experiments on shinobi. A mere military facade, Otogakure was used to attack Konoha during the Chunin Exams and recruit experimental subjects.

An isolated hidden village guarding a hero's liquid capable of temporarily granting extraordinary power. Fuu, its jinchuuriki, was captured by the Akatsuki. Kakuzu originated from there before his defection.
A pacifist village of the Land of Steam that abandoned military ways. Hidan, who originated from there, renounced its values and joined the Akatsuki, contradicting the village's pacifist orientation.

Hidden village of the Land of Sky practicing a unique form of stellar chakra derived from a meteorite. Its intensive training causes fatal damage to users over the long term, revealing internal tension between tradition and survival.
Homeland of the Uzumaki clan, specialists in sealing and longevity, destroyed by a coalition of villages that feared its power in fuinjutsu. Its survivors scattered, with several joining Konoha.
Village of the Land of Snow governed by Doto Kazahana after his murderous coup against his brother. After his defeat by Konoha shinobi, the country began political transformation under Koyuki.
A renegade village whose operatives conducted a series of attacks against Konoha. Its shinobi used advanced elemental techniques and were eliminated in confrontations in isolated areas.
Village specialized in the manufacture of ninja weapons, destroyed by the Five Great Nations who feared its monopoly on armaments. Its survivors sought revenge, particularly against Konoha.
Criminal organization originally founded by Yahiko as a peace movement before being hijacked by Obito and Madara. The Akatsuki systematically captured tailed beasts to realize the Moon's Eye Plan.

Clandestine division of the Anbu created by Danzo Shimura, operating in the shadows of Konoha's government. Root conditioned its members through emotion suppression and conducted disavowable operations in the name of village security.

Elite shinobi corps operating directly under the Hokage's orders for classified missions. The Anbu accomplishes tasks that regular forces cannot assume, including assassinations and counter-intelligence.

factions.name_en: Seven Ninja Swordsmen of the Mist
factions.description_en: Military elite of Kirigakure comprising seven shinobi wielding the legendary swords of the Mist Village. Their political and symbolic influence remained considerable even during periods of group dissolution.
Internal police force of Konoha historically controlled by the Uchiha clan, granting them major institutional power within the village. Dissolved after the Uchiha clan massacre perpetrated by Itachi.

Supreme political body bringing together the leaders of the five great hidden villages. The Council of the Five Kage coordinated the Great Shinobi Alliance during the Fourth Great War to fight Madara Uchiha and Obito.

Secret organization ostensibly led by Jigen, in reality a vessel of Isshiki Otsutsuki, working to cultivate a divine tree to harvest its fruit. Kara conducted global operations aimed at exploiting Earth's chakra resources.

Celestial lineage of chakra cultivators traversing worlds to plant and harvest divine trees. The Otsutsuki are the origin of all terrestrial shinobi chakra through Kaguya, and represent the supreme extraterrestrial threat.

Founding clan of Konoha alongside the Senju, bearers of the Sharingan, it was nearly entirely massacred by Itachi on Danzo's orders. Its strained relationship with Konoha's government fueled coup d'état projects before the massacre.
Founding clan of Konoha, celebrated for its vital techniques and Hashirama's Mokuton. The Senju established the founding alliance with the Uchiha, thus creating the hidden village system.

Clan originating from Uzushiogakure, linked to the Senju by blood, masters of sealing and endowed with exceptional vitality. After the destruction of their village, its members scattered across the nations, some becoming jinchuuriki.

The most influential clan in Konoha after the fall of the Uchiha, bearer of the Byakugan and Jyuken. Its internal structure is divided into main branch and secondary branch, the latter enslaved by a cursed seal.
Konoha clan recognized for its shadow manipulation techniques and sacred deer. The Nara form with the Akimichi and Yamanaka a triadic alliance passed down from generation to generation.
Konoha clan specialized in body transformation and physical expansion techniques. Historical partners of the Nara and Yamanaka, the Akimichi occupy a role as striking force in tripartite formations.

Konoha clan mastering mental transfer and telepathy techniques, playing a crucial role in intelligence and tactical communication. Inoichi Yamanaka served as chief of communications during the Fourth War.

Konoha clan that provided the village with its Third Hokage, a respected patriarchal figure. Hiruzen Sarutobi embodied the ideal of the teacher and politician shinobi, while Asuma served as an elite jonin.

A small clan whose most influential representative was Danzo Shimura, operating in the shadows of Konoha's government. Danzo secretly accumulated Sharingan and manipulated the village's political events for decades.

Konoha clan whose members host Kikaichuu insects from birth and use these creatures as living weapons consuming enemy chakra. Discreet and analytical, the clan plays an intelligence role within the village.
Konoha clan where each member forms a combat duo with a specialized ninja dog. Their combat style based on speed and refined canine senses makes them unparalleled scouts and trackers.

A restricted lineage of Konoha illustrated by the White Fang Sakumo and his son Kakashi, who became the Sixth Hokage. Despite the temporary infamy linked to Sakumo's suicide, Kakashi restored family honor and became a major tutelary figure.

Clan of fanatical warriors bearing the Shikotsumyaku technique allowing them to manipulate their own bones. Nearly exterminated during a reckless attack on Kirigakure, the only notable survivor was Kimimaro, taken in by Orochimaru.

Kirigakure clan mastering the body liquefaction technique Suika no Jutsu. Mangetsu was one of the Seven Ninja Swordsmen while Gengetsu Hozuki served as Second Mizukage, conferring notable political influence on the clan.

Clan of shinobi pirates operating in the waters of the Land of Water, seeking revenge on Kirigakure. Araumi Funato had accumulated power considered a direct threat to the maritime stability of the region.

Cursed clan bearing the Ketsuryugan, a red eye capable of controlling blood. Exiled to the Valley of Hell after political manipulation orchestrated by the Uchiha, the clan harbors deep resentment toward Konoha.

Former clan of feared mercenaries, divided between a branch allied with Konoha and a dissident branch rallied to Orochimaru in the Land of Sound. This internal fracture reflects tensions between tradition and shinobi opportunism.

Sacred place of summoned toads and home of the Sage Art of hermit mode. Maintaining an ancestral link with Konoha through the summoning contracts of the Senju and Uzumaki, Mount Myoboku trained several legendary shinobi including Jiraiya and Naruto.

Mythical forest inhabited by giant summoned slugs, notably Katsuyu. Linked to shinobi medics and students of the Legendary Tsunade, Shikkotsu Forest represents a unique power of healing and support in the shinobi world.

Sacred dwelling of summoned snakes and seat of the Sage Art of snakes. Orochimaru concluded a contract there and trained Yakushi Kabuto in Sage Mode, Ryuchi Cave offering a dark counterpoint to Mount Myoboku.

Exclusively female village whose tradition requires the future leader to challenge and defeat the man she loves. This custom contributes to its political isolation but confers on its warriors a reputation as elite fighters.
Village guardian of a forbidden explosion technique passed down by the Tsuchigumo clan. Protected by an alliance with Konoha, the village was coveted for this technique by several criminal factions.

Order of hermit mode masters residing at Mount Myoboku, transmitting the art of Senjutsu to the rare chosen ones capable of learning it. Their spiritual and prophetic influence guided several crucial decisions in shinobi history.

Founding lineage of all shinobi chakra, descending directly from Kaguya Otsutsuki through the Sage of Six Paths. The conflict between Indra and Ashura initiated the ancestral rivalry perpetuated by the Uchiha and Senju clans.

Village specialized in the creation and management of the Great Pagoda Prison, the most secure detention facility in the shinobi world. Its authority over this prison confers on it a delicate political position between the great nations.
Minor hidden village that participated in Konoha's chunin exams. Karin Uzumaki lived part of her childhood there before joining Orochimaru, illustrating the permeability of its political borders.

Influential clan of Kumogakure whose members occupy the highest military and political positions in the Cloud village. The Fourth Raikage Ay and his adoptive brother Killer Bee are its most emblematic representatives.
